title: Core OSS Platform Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the Core open-source productivity platform — a monorepo with a FastAPI backend, React frontend, and Cloudflare Worker image proxy.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of Python and TypeScript
Familiarity with REST APIs and React

steps.title: Welcome to Core OSS
steps.description: Core is an all-in-one productivity platform providing Email, Calendar, AI Chat, Messages, Files, Documents, Projects, and a unified Dashboard. The monorepo is organized into three packages — a Python backend, a React frontend, and a Cloudflare Worker for image handling.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over each package to see its size and contents

steps.title: Backend: FastAPI Application
steps.description: The backend is built with FastAPI and Python. The highlighted entry point registers all routers, middleware, and error handlers, and deploys as a Vercel serverless function. The API layer contains the HTTP routing and business logic, while the shared libraries hold clients for Supabase, R2, Resend, and AI tools.

interactiveActions.description: Click index.py to see the main FastAPI app setup

steps.title: Backend Architecture: Routers & Services
steps.description: The backend follows a Router, Service, Library pattern. Routers handle HTTP concerns like validation, status codes, and auth. Services contain business logic organized by domain — email, calendar, chat, files, documents, projects, and more. Each domain is a self-contained package with focused modules.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the services directory to see domain-specific packages

steps.title: AI Chat System
steps.description: The AI chat system is one of Core's key features. It uses a tool-calling agent architecture with 12+ tools — email search, calendar operations, web search, semantic search, and more. Messages stream in real time with interleaved text and tool results.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the chat service to see agent and prompt modules

steps.title: Database: Supabase Migrations
steps.description: Core uses Supabase (PostgreSQL) with Row-Level Security for multi-tenant isolation. The 24 sequential migrations here define the full schema — from users and workspaces through email, calendar, chat, projects, and permissions.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over migrations to see the full schema evolution

steps.title: Frontend: React + TypeScript SPA
steps.description: The frontend is a React 19 and TypeScript app built with Vite. It uses Zustand for state, TanStack React Query for data fetching, Tailwind CSS for styling, and TipTap for rich text editing. The 24 highlighted feature modules are each self-contained with their own components, hooks, and types.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the components directory to see all 24 feature modules

steps.title: Frontend Infrastructure: API Client, Hooks & Utilities
steps.description: The frontend's supporting infrastructure — the API client handles auth and streaming, custom hooks manage data fetching, and shared libraries provide clients for Supabase, Sentry, PostHog, and common utilities.

interactiveActions.description: Click on the API client to see how HTTP requests and auth are handled

steps.title: Image Proxy: Cloudflare Worker
steps.description: A lightweight Cloudflare Worker that serves images from R2 storage with HMAC-signed URLs, on-the-fly resizing, and edge caching. The entire implementation fits in a single file, supporting thumb, chat, preview, and full-size variants.

interactiveActions.description: Click index.ts to see the full Worker implementation

steps.title: Testing & CI/CD
steps.description: The backend has a comprehensive test suite organized by feature — unit, chat, search, documents, and prompts. CI/CD is handled via GitHub Actions, where pull requests automatically trigger lint, type-check, test, and build checks. A separate workflow runs Gitleaks for secret scanning.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the test directory to see test organization by feature

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: You've now seen the complete Core OSS platform — backend, frontend, and image proxy. The architecture follows clean separation of HTTP layer, business logic, and shared libraries, making it straightforward to navigate and contribute to.

interactiveActions.description: Freely explore the codebase — click on any building to see its contents
